description: B600-KB2 is the south-distribution KYLA pool with 3 + inputs in Excel: B623.KB1_INT_VERK1, B653.KB2_WVÄRME_ACK, B654.KB2_Pkyl_Ack. Of these: B654 stays flat all year, B653 is an accumulator (not monotonic), and only B623 consistently contributes. Pool topology mirrors Excel, but B600-KB2 readings reflect only ~1/3 of intended inputs. Building-level reconciliation matches Excel within noise because Excel cache uses the same inputs.

B611 kyla = -20.88 MWh Jan / -12.01 MWh Feb is a data quality artifact. Formula: 0.38×B600-KB2 − B631.KB1_INTE_VERK2 − B631.KB1_VMM51_E. With B600-KB2 pool collapsed to just B623 (see campus annotation "b600kb2_pool_dead_inputs"), the 0.38 share is far smaller than the sub-meters being subtracted. Topology matches Excel exactly. Would revert to plausible positive if B653 / B654.KB2 came back online.

B641 kyla = -1.75 MWh Jan / -1.80 MWh Feb in Excel. Formula: 0.1×(B612.KB1_PKYL − B637 − B638). B612.KB1_PKYL is a bi-daily sensor (31 readings over the 59-day window) with zero delta on every recorded day. On days it has no reading, the 0.1× share isn't computed and the B637/B638 scaled subtraction also doesn't fire. Topology reports 0 instead of the Excel −1.75. Structural limitation of views.sql (no fractional hasSubMeter primitive); also exacerbated by bi-daily sensor schedule.

B833 kyla = -0.84 MWh Jan / -1.04 MWh Feb. Formula: B833.KB1_GF4 − B834.KB2_INT_VERK. B833.KB1_GF4 is bi-daily (15 Jan / 16 Feb readings out of 31/28). On days without its reading, B834 subtraction doesn't fire. Topology reports ~half the Excel negative. Would match if the parent had daily readings; fix requires either Snowflake padding (disallowed) or introducing a sensor-interpolation layer.

B658.KB2_VMM51_E reports ~10-12 MWh/month of real cooling in Snowflake, but Excel cache for B658 kyla = 0 (STRUX_data value is stale/zero). Same pattern as B616 steam and B665.T42-2-1 EL — known "Excel=0 but meter live" misallocation. Topology reports the real consumption.

B623.KB1_INT_VERK1 is a +term for B623 AND an input to the B600-KB2 virtual aggregator. Excel counts it in both accounting views (conservation-violating). Topology must pick one: we route it via feeds to B600-KB2 so 611/613/621/622 shares are correct, which zeroes out B623's own-building contribution. Trade-off: B623 building shows 0 in topology vs 3.67/5.06 MWh in Excel.

Excel row 14 (B612) subtracts B637 and B638 at k=0.9; row 29 (B641) subtracts them at k=0.1. views.sql's hasSubMeter only supports k=1 full subtraction. Topology subtracts B637/B638 from B612 at k=1 (gap −1.75 MWh/month) and doesn't subtract from B641 (gap +1.75 MWh/month). These offset at campus level. Closing the gap requires a fractional-subtraction primitive.

Meter reports power (kW). Excel row 26 converts to MWh via *24*31/1000 (literal 31-day month assumption; coefficient = 0.744). Encoded as feeds B634.KB1_PKYL → B634.KYLA_VIRT at k=0.744, with the residual 0.256 going to a campus-attributed virtual B634.KYLA_LOSSES so outgoing feeds sum to 1.0 (validator requirement). Matches Excel Jan; Feb will be ~11% high vs physical reality because Excel uses the 31-day constant for all months.
Excel row 50: B821 = B821.KB2_VMM51 − 0.8 × B841.KB2_VM51. Fractional subtraction not topologically encoded; skipped because both meters report 0 delta during Jan-Feb 2026. Revisit when active.

Prod-600 is an Excel accounting virtual (total chilled-water production) that sums 4 source meters (B653, B654.KB1, B654.KB2, B661.KB1_Pkyl_Ack) at k=1. It has no downstream dependents. Not modeled in topology to avoid double-counting B653 and B654.KB2 which also feed B600-KB2. Preserved in meter_allocations.csv for documentation.

B612.KB1_PKYL and B833.KB1_GF4 are bi-daily BMS sensors (reading only every other day — 31 readings over 59-day Jan+Feb window). Any hasSubMeter edge with these as parent will only subtract child flow on the days the parent has a reading. Affects B612/B641 (via B612.KB1_PKYL) and B833 (via B833.KB1_GF4). The gaps are fractions of the Excel values: B833 topology reports ~half the expected negative.
Kyla drift of ~4.1% vs Excel, both Jan and Feb 2026. Cause not yet classified. Flagged for diagnosis.
Kyla drift of ~4.1% vs Excel, both Jan and Feb 2026. Cause not yet classified. Same pattern as B621; likely shared root cause.

Reference formula doc (KYLA sheet) defines GTN building formulas using a different meter set than the current ontology. Doc uses KB1/KB2_VMM50_E + KB1_VMM51_E style meters (all confirmed present in Snowflake). Current ontology uses KB1_INT_VERK, KB1_PKYL, KB1_KylEffekt_Ack, KB2_Pkyl_Ack and virtual pool meters (B600-KB2, Prod-600, per-building .KYLA_VIRT). Current ontology delivers 86 of 98 building-months matching Excel cache — i.e. Excel accounting uses the same meter set as our ontology, not the doc's VMM50_E meters. Doc's formulas, if applied verbatim, would regress the reconciliation. Likely the doc reflects a planned meter-renaming pass that has not yet propagated to Excel. Retained current allocations; flagged doc divergence for upstream verification.

Reference formula doc marks ALL SNV buildings as N/A for KYLA and notes 'STÄNGDA HUS RÄKNAS SOM ETT' for the closed-house group (B202/203/204/205/209). However, current SNV KYLA ontology has 22 buildings with real allocations and data (derived from the existing SNV Excel workbook, snv_kyla workstream). The doc appears to be incomplete / not yet authoritative for SNV KYLA. Current ontology retained; doc status noted.

Four GTN KYLA meters are flat at zero for the entire reference period: B638.KB1_INT_VERK1, B643.KB1_INT_VERK, B821.KB2_VMM51, B841.KB2_VM51. Either dead counters or cooling branches that never activated. Excel cache mostly = 0 for these buildings' KYLA so impact on reconciliation is minimal. Flagged for on-site verification.

Several GTN KYLA meters have the '_Ack' suffix (Ackumulerat = accumulated) and legitimately go up and down — they are state accumulators, not monotonic counters. Examples: B661.KB1_Pkyl_Ack (34 negative deltas), B653.KB2_WVÄRME_ACK, B674.KB1_PKYL2_Ack, B654.KB2_Pkyl_Ack, B654.KB1_KylEffekt_Ack. Their readings reflect accumulator state, not cumulative energy. The delta-based anomaly scanner flags these but they are by design.
B642.KB1_INT_VERK_1 flat at zero for 252 days ending 2025-09-10. Possibly decommissioned or physically disconnected; picked up activity only very late in the period. B642 KYLA Excel value is minimal.

B661.KB1_INTVERK flat for 218 days ending 2025-08-07 (first 7 months of 2025). Large-magnitude meter in active season (median 0.4 MWh/day but bursty to 3 MWh/day). Likely commissioned mid-2025 or prior gateway outage. Not patched; historical gap noted.

B833.KB1_GF4 shows a 90-day max flat run but bursts to 986 MWh/day. This is the bi-daily BMS polling pattern already noted in b833_negative_sparse — the meter reports on a sparse cadence (~31 reads over 59 days). Cumulative is correct; per-day view shows bursts. See b833_negative_sparse for the downstream implications.

B612.KB1_PKYL shows bursty reporting (88-day max flat, max 1556 MWh/day delta). Likely same sparse-polling pattern as B833.KB1_GF4. Cumulative totals remain correct; daily-resolution views show jumps.
B631.KB1_VMM51_E had a 119-day flat ending 2025-09-30 plus 1 negative delta event. Could be a gauge reset or transient offline. Small absolute impact (median 0.2 MWh/day). Flagged for context.
B658.KB2_VMM51_E flat 70 days ending 2025-03-26. Low-magnitude cooling meter; pre-season dormancy pattern common to chilled-water sub-meters. Accepted.
B623.KB1_INT_VERK1 flat 34 days ending 2026-01-09. Low-magnitude (median 0.16 MWh/day). Winter dormancy on a cooling circuit is expected. Accepted.
